instruction:
Calculate the following integral:
$$
\int_{0}^{+\infty} {\frac{\operatorname{s i n} x} {x ( x^{2}+1 )^{2}}} \mathrm{d} x
$$

Options:
A. $$
{\frac{\pi} {2}} \left( 1-{\frac{3} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
B. $$
{\frac{\pi} {2}} \left( 1-{\frac{3} {\mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
C. $$
{\frac{\pi} {2}} \left( 1-{\frac{2} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
D. $$
{\frac{\pi} {2}} \left( 1-{\frac{3} {4 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
E. $$
{\frac{\pi} {4}} \left( 1-{\frac{3} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
F. $$
{\frac{\pi^{2}} {2}} \left( 1-{\frac{3} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
G. $$
{\frac{\pi} {2}} \left( 2-{\frac{3} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
H. $$
{\frac{\pi} {2}} \left( 1+{\frac{3} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
I. $$
{\frac{\pi} {6}} \left( 1-{\frac{3} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
J. $$
{\frac{\pi} {3}} \left( 1-{\frac{5} {2 \mathrm{e}}} \right)
$$
